    1457                 :            : 
    1458                 :      70617 :                 relation = heap_open(cache->cc_reloid, AccessShareLock);
    1459                 :            : 
    1460                 :      70617 :                 scandesc = systable_beginscan(relation,
    1461                 :      70617 :                                                                           cache->cc_indexoid,
    1462                 :      70617 :                                                                           IndexScanOK(cache, cur_skey),
    1463                 :            :                                                                           SnapshotNow,

    1471                 :            :                 {
    1472                 :            :                         uint32          hashValue;

    1474                 :            :

    1500                 :            :                                  */

    1503                 :            : 
    1504                 :     146652 :                                 break;                  /* A-OK */

    1506                 :            :

    1517                 :     349080 :                         ctlist = lappend(ctlist, ct);
    1518                 :     349080 :                         ct->refcount++;
    1519                 :            :                 }
    1520                 :            : 
    1521                 :      70617 :                 systable_endscan(scandesc);

    1524                 :            : 
    1525                 :            :                 /*

    1556                 :            : #ifndef CATCACHE_FORCE_RELEASE
    1557 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ct->dead &&
    1558                 :            : #endif

    1560         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 (ct->c_list == NULL || ct->c_list->refcount == 0))

    1571                 :      70617 :         cl->refcount = 0;                    /* for the moment */
    1572                 :      70617 :         cl->dead = false;

    1605                 :            : /*
    1606                 :            :  *      ReleaseCatCacheList
    1607                 :            :  *
    1608                 :            :  *      Decrement the reference count of a catcache list.

    1610                 :            : void
    1611                 :            : ReleaseCatCacheList(CatCList *list)
    1612                 :            : {
    1613                 :            :         /* Safety checks to ensure we were handed a cache entry */
    1614 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:    1236721 :         Assert(list->cl_magic == CL_MAGIC);
    1615 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:    1236721 :         Assert(list->refcount > 0);
    1616                 :    1236721 :         list->refcount--;
    1617                 :    1236721 :         ResourceOwnerForgetCatCacheListRef(CurrentResourceOwner, list);
    1618                 :            : 
    1619                 :            :         if (
    1620                 :            : #ifndef CATCACHE_FORCE_RELEASE
    1621 [ +  + ][ +  - ]:    1236721 :                 list->dead &&
    1622                 :            : #endif

    1625                 :    1236721 : }

    1627                 :            : 
    1628                 :            : /*
    1629                 :            :  * CatalogCacheCreateEntry
    1630                 :            :  *              Create a new CatCTup entry, copying the given HeapTuple and other
    1631                 :            :  *              supplied data into it.  The new entry initially has refcount 0.
    1632                 :            :  */
    1633                 :            : static CatCTup *
    1634                 :            : CatalogCacheCreateEntry(CatCache *cache, HeapTuple ntp,
    1635                 :            :                                                 uint32 hashValue, Index hashIndex, bool negative)
    1636                 :            : {
    1637                 :            :         CatCTup    *ct;
    1638                 :            :         HeapTuple       dtp;
    1639                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
    1640                 :            : 
    1641                 :            :         /*
    1642                 :            :          * If there are any out-of-line toasted fields in the tuple, expand them
    1643                 :            :          * in-line.  This saves cycles during later use of the catcache entry,
    1644                 :            :          * and also protects us against the possibility of the toast tuples being
    1645                 :            :          * freed before we attempt to fetch them, in case of something using a
    1646                 :            :          * slightly stale catcache entry.
    1647                 :            :          */
    1648         [ -  + ]:    2641103 :         if (HeapTupleHasExternal(ntp))
    1649                 :          0 :                 dtp = toast_flatten_tuple(ntp, cache->cc_tupdesc);
    1650                 :            :         else
    1651                 :    2641103 :                 dtp = ntp;
    1652                 :            : 
    1653                 :            :         /*
    1654                 :            :          * Allocate CatCTup header in cache memory, and copy the tuple there too.
    1655                 :            :          */
    1656                 :    2641103 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
    1657                 :    2641103 :         ct = (CatCTup *) palloc(sizeof(CatCTup));
    1658                 :    2641103 :         heap_copytuple_with_tuple(dtp, &ct->tuple);
    1659                 :    2641103 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
    1660                 :            : 
    1661         [ -  + ]:    2641103 :         if (dtp != ntp)
    1662                 :          0 :                 heap_freetuple(dtp);
    1663                 :            : 
    1664                 :            :         /*
    1665                 :            :          * Finish initializing the CatCTup header, and add it to the cache's
    1666                 :            :          * linked list and counts.
    1667                 :            :          */
    1668                 :    2641103 :         ct->ct_magic = CT_MAGIC;
    1669                 :    2641103 :         ct->my_cache = cache;
    1670                 :    2641103 :         DLInitElem(&ct->cache_elem, (void *) ct);
    1671                 :    2641103 :         ct->c_list = NULL;
    1672                 :    2641103 :         ct->refcount = 0;                    /* for the moment */
    1673                 :    2641103 :         ct->dead = false;
    1674                 :    2641103 :         ct->negative = negative;
    1675                 :    2641103 :         ct->hash_value = hashValue;
    1676                 :            : 
    1677                 :    2641103 :         DLAddHead(&cache->cc_bucket[hashIndex], &ct->cache_elem);
    1678                 :            : 
    1679                 :    2641103 :         cache->cc_ntup++;
    1680                 :    2641103 :         CacheHdr->ch_ntup++;
    1681                 :            : 
    1682                 :    2641103 :         return ct;
    1683                 :            : }
    1684                 :            : 
    1685                 :            : /*
    1686                 :            :  * build_dummy_tuple
    1687                 :            :  *              Generate a palloc'd HeapTuple that contains the specified key
    1688                 :            :  *              columns, and NULLs for other columns.
    1689                 :            :  *
    1690                 :            :  * This is used to store the keys for negative cache entries and CatCList
    1691                 :            :  * entries, which don't have real tuples associated with them.
    1692                 :            :  */
    1693                 :            : static HeapTuple
    1694                 :            : build_dummy_tuple(CatCache *cache, int nkeys, ScanKey skeys)
    1695                 :            : {
    1696                 :            :         HeapTuple       ntp;
    1697                 :     775084 :         TupleDesc       tupDesc = cache->cc_tupdesc;
    1698                 :            :         Datum      *values;
    1699                 :            :         bool       *nulls;
    1700                 :     775084 :         Oid                     tupOid = InvalidOid;
    1701                 :            :         NameData        tempNames[4];
    1702                 :            :         int                     i;
    1703                 :            : 
    1704                 :     775084 :         values = (Datum *) palloc(tupDesc->natts * sizeof(Datum));
    1705                 :     775084 :         nulls = (bool *) palloc(tupDesc->natts * sizeof(bool));
    1706                 :            : 
    1707                 :     775084 :         memset(values, 0, tupDesc->natts * sizeof(Datum));
    1708                 :     775084 :         memset(nulls, true, tupDesc->natts * sizeof(bool));
    1709                 :            : 
    1710         [ +  + ]:    2155832 :         for (i = 0; i < nkeys; i++)
    1711                 :            :         {
    1712                 :    1380748 :                 int                     attindex = cache->cc_key[i];
    1713                 :    1380748 :                 Datum           keyval = skeys[i].sk_argument;
    1714                 :            : 
    1715         [ +  + ]:    1380748 :                 if (attindex > 0)
    1716                 :            :                 {
    1717                 :            :                         /*
    1718                 :            :                          * Here we must be careful in case the caller passed a C string
    1719                 :            :                          * where a NAME is wanted: convert the given argument to a
    1720                 :            :                          * correctly padded NAME.  Otherwise the memcpy() done in
    1721                 :            :                          * heap_form_tuple could fall off the end of memory.
    1722                 :            :                          */
    1723         [ +  + ]:    1282833 :                         if (cache->cc_isname[i])
    1724                 :            :                         {
    1725                 :     370398 :                                 Name            newval = &tempNames[i];
    1726                 :            : 
    1727                 :     370398 :                                 namestrcpy(newval, DatumGetCString(keyval));
    1728                 :     370398 :                                 keyval = NameGetDatum(newval);
    1729                 :     370398 :                         }
    1730                 :    1282833 :                         values[attindex - 1] = keyval;
    1731                 :    1282833 :                         nulls[attindex - 1] = false;
    1732                 :    1282833 :                 }
    1733                 :            :                 else
    1734                 :            :                 {
    1735 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:      97915 :                         Assert(attindex == ObjectIdAttributeNumber);
    1736                 :      97915 :                         tupOid = DatumGetObjectId(keyval);
    1737                 :            :                 }
    1738                 :    1380748 :         }
    1739                 :            : 
    1740                 :     775084 :         ntp = heap_form_tuple(tupDesc, values, nulls);
    1741         [ +  + ]:     775084 :         if (tupOid != InvalidOid)
    1742 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:      97225 :                 HeapTupleSetOid(ntp, tupOid);
    1743                 :            : 
    1744                 :     775084 :         pfree(values);
    1745                 :     775084 :         pfree(nulls);
    1746                 :            : 
    1747                 :     775084 :         return ntp;
    1748                 :            : }
    1749                 :            : 
    1750                 :            : 
    1751                 :            : /*
    1752                 :            :  *      PrepareToInvalidateCacheTuple()
    1753                 :            :  *
    1754                 :            :  *      This is part of a rather subtle chain of events, so pay attention:
    1755                 :            :  *
    1756                 :            :  *      When a tuple is inserted or deleted, it cannot be flushed from the
    1757                 :            :  *      catcaches immediately, for reasons explained at the top of cache/inval.c.
    1758                 :            :  *      Instead we have to add entry(s) for the tuple to a list of pending tuple
    1759                 :            :  *      invalidations that will be done at the end of the command or transaction.
    1760                 :            :  *
    1761                 :            :  *      The lists of tuples that need to be flushed are kept by inval.c.  This
    1762                 :            :  *      routine is a helper routine for inval.c.  Given a tuple belonging to
    1763                 :            :  *      the specified relation, find all catcaches it could be in, compute the
    1764                 :            :  *      correct hash value for each such catcache, and call the specified function
    1765                 :            :  *      to record the cache id, hash value, and tuple ItemPointer in inval.c's
    1766                 :            :  *      lists.  CatalogCacheIdInvalidate will be called later, if appropriate,
    1767                 :            :  *      using the recorded information.
    1768                 :            :  *
    1769                 :            :  *      Note that it is irrelevant whether the given tuple is actually loaded
    1770                 :            :  *      into the catcache at the moment.  Even if it's not there now, it might
    1771                 :            :  *      be by the end of the command, or there might be a matching negative entry
    1772                 :            :  *      to flush --- or other backends' caches might have such entries --- so
    1773                 :            :  *      we have to make list entries to flush it later.
    1774                 :            :  *
    1775                 :            :  *      Also note that it's not an error if there are no catcaches for the
    1776                 :            :  *      specified relation.  inval.c doesn't know exactly which rels have
    1777                 :            :  *      catcaches --- it will call this routine for any tuple that's in a
    1778                 :            :  *      system relation.
    1779                 :            :  */
    1780                 :            : void
    1781                 :            : PrepareToInvalidateCacheTuple(Relation relation,
    1782                 :            :                                                           HeapTuple tuple,
    1783                 :            :                                                         void (*function) (int, uint32, ItemPointer, Oid))
    1784                 :            : {
    1785                 :            :         CatCache   *ccp;
    1786                 :            :         Oid                     reloid;
    1787                 :            :
